description: Self-hosted Personal DevOps Workspace Platform for Ubuntu Desktop
completedTasks: Create the complete directory structure
Initialize the Go module
Initialize Git
Create all documentation files under docs/
Create docs/PROJECT_STATE.json
Create .gitignore
Create Makefile
Create LICENSE (MIT)
Create README.md
Verify the repository builds
Implement Workspace domain model (internal/workspace)
Implement disk-backed workspace.Manager (Create/Get/List/Delete)
Implement internal/engine.Engine orchestration layer
Implement internal/config for path resolution
Implement internal/utils.NewID
Add unit tests for config, utils, workspace, engine
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 1)
Implement Template domain model and Registry (internal/template)
Seed templates/ with kubernetes, docker, jenkins, linux, terraform, ansible definitions
Add TemplatesDir to internal/config
Wire Engine.CreateWorkspace to resolve Services from a Template
Add Engine.ListTemplates / Engine.GetTemplate
Add unit tests for template.Registry; update engine tests
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 2)
Implement internal/storage.Open (SQLite, pure Go, no cgo)
Add DatabasePath to internal/config
Add SQLite index to workspace.Manager (name uniqueness, ordered List)
Update workspace.NewManager to accept *sql.DB and return error
Keep index in sync with workspace.json on Create/Delete, with rollback
Add modernc.org/sqlite dependency; go mod tidy
Add unit tests for internal/storage; update workspace/engine tests to use real SQLite
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 3)
Define shared Runtime interface and Command/Result types (internal/runtime)
Implement Shell Runtime (internal/runtime/shell) backed by os/exec.CommandContext
Fix context-cancellation vs nonzero-exit-code bug found during testing
Add unit tests for internal/runtime/shell
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 4)
Implement k3d Runtime (internal/runtime/k3d) composed over runtime.Runtime
Constrain k3d Runtime Execute to the k3d binary only
Add CreateCluster/StartCluster/StopCluster/DeleteCluster/ListClusters/ClusterExists
Add unit tests for internal/runtime/k3d using a fake runtime.Runtime
Manually verify ListClusters JSON parsing against a real k3d cluster (one-off, not automated)
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 5)
Implement Docker Runtime (internal/runtime/docker) composed over runtime.Runtime
Constrain Docker Runtime Execute to the docker binary only
Add CreateContainer/StartContainer/StopContainer/RemoveContainer/ContainerStatus/ContainerExists/ContainerLogs
Add ErrNotFound/ErrAlreadyExists classified from real docker CLI stderr text
Manually verify command shapes and error text against a real docker instance (one-off, not automated)
Add unit tests for internal/runtime/docker using a fake runtime.Runtime
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 6)
Define Service interface, Status type, ErrNotFound (internal/service)
Implement Kubernetes Service (internal/service/kubernetes) backed by a k3d cluster
Extend internal/runtime/k3d with ErrAlreadyExists and GetKubeconfig
Add unit tests composing real k3d.Runtime/docker.Runtime over a shared fake runtime.Runtime
Manually verify full Kubernetes Service lifecycle against real k3d/docker (one-off, not automated)
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 7)
Implement Docker Service (internal/service/docker) backed directly by docker.Runtime
Reuse dockerruntime.ContainerSpec as the Docker Service construction parameter
Map docker inspect state to service.Status, including the distinct created state
Add unit tests composing a real docker.Runtime over a fake runtime.Runtime
Manually verify full Docker Service lifecycle against real docker (one-off, not automated)
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 8)
Implement Jenkins Service (internal/service/jenkins) by embedding servicedocker.Service
Override Create to prepare the host data directory (JENKINS_HOME bind mount)
Add InitialAdminPassword, reading directly from the bind-mounted host path
Manually verify official Jenkins image has no bind-mount permission issue (one-off, not automated)
Add unit tests for Jenkins-specific behavior plus a delegation check
Manually verify full Jenkins Service lifecycle with a real Jenkins boot (one-off, not automated)
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 9)
Ask user how the generic docker Service should behave by default (Docker-in-Docker chosen)
Add service type catalog (service.KnownTypes/IsKnownType, all six Service Rules examples)
Wire template.Registry.Load to validate services entries against the catalog (ErrUnknownService)
Implement internal/service/factory to build a Service from a type name + workspace context
Add ContainerSpec.Privileged and docker.RemoveVolume; verify dind requirements live
Shorten utils.NewID to 12 hex chars to fit k3d's 32-character cluster name limit (discovered live)
Switch dind storage to a named Docker volume after discovering a bind-mount permission issue live
Add workspace.Manager.SetStatus and DataDir
Wire internal/engine/lifecycle.go: StartWorkspace/StopWorkspace/ResetWorkspace/WorkspaceStatus/WorkspaceLogs
Make DeleteWorkspace context-aware and clean up Services before the Workspace record
Add unit tests for every new/changed piece
Manually verify the complete real lifecycle end-to-end for kubernetes and docker templates (one-off, not automated)
Verify the repository builds (Sprint 10)
notes: cmd/devlab/main.go remains a minimal entrypoint; CLI/API wiring to actually call the Engine is Sprint 11, which is next. engine.New now takes (*workspace.Manager, *template.Registry, *factory.Factory). CreateWorkspace resolves Services from the Template but does not provision them (lazy provisioning, ADR-0025) — StartWorkspace does, idempotently. workspace.NewManager takes (rootDir string, db *sql.DB) and returns (*Manager, error); workspace.json remains the source of truth for full Workspace data, SQLite is only a name-uniqueness/ordering index (see ADR-0010, ADR-0011); Manager also has SetStatus and DataDir now. internal/runtime.Runtime (Execute(ctx, Command) (*Result, error)) is the shared contract for Shell/Docker/k3d Runtimes (ADR-0012); no command allow-list on Shell Runtime, injection prevented structurally via argv slices (ADR-0013). k3d Runtime and Docker Runtime are composed over an injected runtime.Runtime and restrict Execute to their own single binary (ADR-0014, ADR-0015); Docker Runtime gained Privileged and RemoveVolume this sprint (ADR-0022, ADR-0024). internal/service.Service (Create/Start/Stop/Reset/Delete/Status/Logs) is the shared contract for all Services, with its own Status decoupled from workspace.Status; internal/service also now owns the service type catalog (KnownTypes/IsKnownType, ADR-0020), used by both internal/template (validation) and internal/service/factory (construction) without creating an import cycle. Kubernetes Service uses both k3d.Runtime and docker.Runtime (ADR-0016). Docker Service uses only docker.Runtime and introduced service.StatusCreated (ADR-0018). Jenkins Service embeds Docker Service (ADR-0019). internal/service/factory.Build maps a service type + workspace ID + data dir to a concrete Service: kubernetes -> kubernetes.New; docker -> Docker-in-Docker, privileged, named-volume storage (ADR-0021, ADR-0022, ADR-0024); jenkins -> jenkins.New with a dynamically allocated free port; linux/terraform/ansible -> a clear not-implemented-yet error. utils.NewID is now 12 hex chars (was 32), discovered necessary live because k3d rejects cluster names over 32 characters and devlab-<id>-kubernetes would otherwise be 50 (ADR-0023). internal/engine/lifecycle.go implements StartWorkspace/StopWorkspace/ResetWorkspace/WorkspaceStatus/WorkspaceLogs; DeleteWorkspace is now context-aware and deletes Services before the Workspace record (ADR-0025). The complete real lifecycle (Create/Start/Status/Logs/Stop/Start/Reset/Delete) was verified end-to-end against live k3d/docker for both kubernetes and docker templates, with zero leftover resources after Delete.
